facts.title: Gyeongbokgung Palace
facts.fact: Gyeongbokgung, built in 1395, is Seoul's grand main palace. Visitors who wear hanbok, traditional dress, get in free.
facts.note: Catch the changing-of-the-guard ceremony (**수문장 교대식**) at the main gate.

facts.title: Bukchon Hanok Village
facts.fact: Bukchon Hanok Village preserves hundreds of traditional tile-roof houses on hilly lanes between two palaces — a favorite spot for photos in hanbok.
facts.korean_en: A hanok is a traditional Korean house.
facts.note: A **한옥** uses under-floor heating called 온돌 to stay warm in winter.

facts.title: Busan by the sea
facts.fact: Busan, the second-largest city, is a coastal hub famous for Haeundae Beach, the huge Jagalchi fish market, and a major international film festival.
facts.note: The KTX train from Seoul to **부산** takes about two and a half hours.

facts.title: Bulguksa Temple
facts.fact: Bulguksa in Gyeongju is one of Korea's most famous Buddhist temples, built during the Silla kingdom. Its stone bridges and pagodas are a UNESCO World Heritage site.
facts.note: Gyeongju (**경주**) was the capital of the Silla kingdom for almost a thousand years.

facts.title: Seokguram Grotto
facts.fact: Seokguram, on a mountain above Bulguksa, is a man-made stone grotto holding a large seated Buddha. The statue faces east toward the sea to greet the sunrise.
facts.korean_en: I watch the sunrise at Seokguram.
facts.note: **석굴암** and 불국사 are listed together as one UNESCO World Heritage site.

facts.title: Suwon Hwaseong Fortress
facts.fact: Hwaseong in Suwon is a large 18th-century fortress wall built by King Jeongjo. You can walk the whole wall and see gates, watchtowers, and a floodgate over a stream.
facts.korean_en: Suwon Hwaseong is a big fortress.
facts.note: **수원** is also famous for grilled galbi (갈비), short ribs.

facts.title: Jeonju Hanok Village
facts.fact: Jeonju has a large hanok village with hundreds of traditional houses. The city is also the home of bibimbap and is known as a center of Korean food.
facts.korean_en: Jeonju is famous for its food.
facts.note: **전주** is a UNESCO City of Gastronomy, famous for bibimbap.

facts.title: Andong Hahoe Village
facts.fact: Hahoe Village in Andong is an old folk village where people still live in traditional houses. It is famous for a mask dance and is a UNESCO World Heritage site.
facts.korean_en: There is an old village in Andong.
facts.note: Andong (**안동**) is also known for its salted mackerel and soju.

facts.title: Templestay
facts.fact: A templestay lets visitors stay overnight at a Buddhist temple. You can try meditation, eat simple temple food, and wake up early for the morning service.
facts.korean_en: I sleep one night at the temple.
facts.note: A **템플스테이** usually includes meditation (명상) and tea with a monk.

facts.title: N Seoul Tower
facts.fact: N Seoul Tower stands on Namsan mountain in the middle of the city. You can see all of Seoul from the top, and couples leave love locks on the fence.
facts.korean_en: You can see all of Seoul from Namsan Tower.
facts.note: Take the cable car (**케이블카**) up Namsan, or climb the stairs for exercise.

facts.title: Nami Island
facts.fact: Nami Island is a small river island near Chuncheon famous for long tree-lined paths. It became very popular after a hit Korean TV drama was filmed there.
facts.note: You reach **남이섬** by a short ferry ride from near Chuncheon (춘천).

facts.title: Boseong Green Tea Fields
facts.fact: Boseong in the south is famous for green tea grown on steep, striped hillsides. Visitors walk between the rows and drink fresh green tea and even tea ice cream.
facts.korean_en: Boseong is famous for green tea.
facts.note: The green rows of **보성** are most beautiful in late spring and early summer.

facts.title: Cherry Blossom Season
facts.fact: In early April, cherry trees bloom all over Korea. Jinhae near Busan holds the biggest cherry blossom festival, with streets covered in pink flowers.
facts.note: The famous **벚꽃** festival in Jinhae (진해) is held every April.

facts.title: Autumn Foliage at Naejangsan
facts.fact: In autumn, Korea's mountains turn red and yellow. Naejangsan National Park is one of the most popular places to see the bright fall leaves.
facts.korean_en: The autumn leaves are beautiful in fall.
facts.note: Hiking (**등산**) to see 단풍 is a favorite autumn activity in Korea.

facts.title: DMZ Tour
facts.fact: The DMZ is the border zone between South and North Korea. On a guided tour you can visit observation points and look across the border into the North.
facts.korean_en: The DMZ is between South Korea and North Korea.
facts.note: You must join an official tour (**투어**) to visit the **디엠지** safely.

facts.title: Changdeokgung Secret Garden
facts.fact: Changdeokgung is a palace known for its Secret Garden, a large wooded park with ponds and pavilions behind the buildings. You can only enter the garden on a guided tour.
facts.note: The Secret Garden (**후원**) of 창덕궁 is a UNESCO World Heritage site.

facts.title: Jjimjilbang Korean Spa
facts.fact: A jjimjilbang is a Korean spa open day and night. People bathe, relax in hot and cold sauna rooms, eat snacks, and can even sleep over for a low price.
facts.korean_en: I take a bath at the jjimjilbang.
facts.note: At a **찜질방** people often eat baked eggs and sweet rice drink (식혜).

facts.title: Jeju Island
facts.fact: Jeju is a volcanic island south of the mainland and Korea's most popular holiday spot. It has Hallasan, the country's tallest mountain, plus beaches, waterfalls, and lava tubes.
facts.note: Many Koreans fly to **제주도** for a honeymoon or a short holiday.
